creator: portmaster@bsdforge.com
text:
Greetings, Mathieu, and thank you for your reply.
> Hi,
>
> Stagint is not something we're going to remove, ever.

Sorry, I probably should have been more informative on this part. I actually
appreciate what staging brings. My real gripe with it, is that it is ON, by
default. Had several issues with it, this PR being the most recent.

> Plus, NO_STAGE is not
> a variable you can set, it's going to wreck a lot of things.

Has not been my experience. In fact, quite the opposite. As to:
NO_STAGE is not a variable you can set...

From: /usr/ports/UPDATING
20130923:
AUTHOR: bapt@FreeBSD.org

  The ports tree is now staged by default. With pkgng the sequence hasn't
  changed, the main difference is that creating package is now independent
  from installing it. With pkg_install, the package is now created first
  and make install, do install the package.

  New macros:
  - STAGEDIR: PATH to the directory where the port will be staged.
  - NO_STAGE: Keep the old behaviour of the ports tree (aka no staging area)

  Unsupported macro if NO_STAGE is not set:
  - MAN* with staging man page compression and handling of hardlinks and
  symlinks is automatically done in the stage. the manpages becomes then a
  "normal" plist files and should be tracked in pkg-plist.
  - MANCOMPRESSED the compress-man target is able to only compress when it
  needed.

  New target:
  - stage: this installs everything into the stage directory
  - makeplist: this will create a pkg-plist and print it to stdout. This is
  a sample plist and it should always be _reviewed_ not directly used.

  NOTE: with staging only what is in the plist will be installed, nothing more,
  meaning a port staged cannot have leftovers except directories left. It is
  really important to double check the pkg-plist to make sure all the files
  the maintainer wants to package are in! make makeplist can help in that area.

Which is where I found out about it, when I began experiencing issues w/it.
There are other (more recent) remarks regarding NO_STAGE, as well.

>
> I can't reproduce your problem with a new, plain, vanilla setup on my dev
> box, and it also builds fine in poudriere, which is used to generate our
> packages.

Interesting. The box I'm building, is intended to become a
Pointyhat/Tinderbox. It's the first fresh install I've performed in years,
as all of my other servers have been tracking -STABLE for the past many
years. This install comes from the 8.4-dvd1 DVD. Immediately after the
install, I bounced the box, deleted /usr/src && /usr/ports. Performed
portsnap fetch && portsnap extract. Cobbled a new kernel. Built, and
installed world/kernel. Then began building/installing requisite ports.
Failing on this one. I should also add, I have no make.conf(5). src.conf(5)
contains only WITHOUT_CLANG=true. Deleting src.conf(5) had no affect.
In summary; fresh install of 8.4-STABLE, src, and ports r260592
(2014-01-13), on an i386.
The ONLY way I could successfully install dns/yadifa, was after appending
-DNO_STAGE to make, during both make && install.
I also saw PR 185897, had similar, or same issue, back in November. How
was this solved?

>
> What do you have in your environment, in your make.conf, etc... That could
> make things go wrong ?

See (just) above.

>
> Regards,
>
> --
> Mathieu Arnold

Thank you for all your time, and consideration in this matter.

--Chris

>
